messages.content:
You receive an email that contains the external tag. Which of the following should raise your suspicions about the legitimacy of the email?
(Select all that apply)
The email references internal Accenture processes.The email references internal Accenture processes.
The email contains an embedded video from an Accenture leader.The email contains an embedded video from an Accenture leader.
The email contains content that feels unusual for the sender to be sending.The email contains content that feels unusual for the sender to be sending.
The email requests you to call a phone number to resolve a payment issue.The email requests you to call a phone number to resolve a payment issue.

The email references internal Accenture processes.
The email requests you to call a phone number to resolve a payment issue.

Which of the following is a phishing indicator?(Select all that apply)
The email contains emotional motivators.The email contains emotional motivators.
Any unexpected link that starts with “https://”.Any unexpected link that starts with “https://”.
The email comes from within Accenture.The email comes from within Accenture.
The email does not contain spelling errors.The email does not contain spelling errors.

The email contains emotional motivators.
Any unexpected link that starts with “https://”.

What is a sign that an audio or clip was created using deepfake technology?(Select all that apply)
There are unnatural distortions in the audio.There are unnatural distortions in the audio.
The speaker is talking about a subject that you’re not familiar with.The speaker is talking about a subject that you’re not familiar with.
The speaker’s mouth and eye movement are unnatural.The speaker’s mouth and eye movement are unnatural.
The audio contains longer-than-usual pauses between words and sentences.The audio contains longer-than-usual pauses between words and sentences.
The speaker’s mouth and eye movement are unnatural.

What is a trustworthy location to find QR codes?(Select all that apply)
On a restaurant’s menu.On a restaurant’s menu.
On a public bulletin board.On a public bulletin board.
On a sticker that is stuck to a streetlightOn a sticker that is stuck to a streetlight
On unsolicited paper mail.On unsolicited paper mail.
On a restaurant’s menu.

Richard receives a social media direct message from an unknown sender. The message contains a link to a “flash sale” claiming to sell legitimate merchandise at discounted prices. What should Richard do?(Select all that apply)
Delete the message.Delete the message.
Click the link to ensure he doesn’t miss out on the sale.Click the link to ensure he doesn’t miss out on the sale.
Reply to the message asking for additional details about the sale.Reply to the message asking for additional details about the sale.
Follow the account to ensure he doesn’t miss any future sales.Follow the account to ensure he doesn’t miss any future sales.
Delete the message.

What is a sign that a profile on social media is not legitimate?(Select all that apply)
The profile picture is blank.The profile picture is blank.
The profile has recently created many posts.The profile has recently created many posts.
The profile is a duplicate of someone you are already connected to.The profile is a duplicate of someone you are already connected to.
The profile picture is something other than the person’s face.The profile picture is something other than the person’s face.

The profile picture is blank.
The profile has recently created many posts.
The profile picture is something other than the person’s face.

When do you need to call ASOC?(Select all that apply)
You received a phishing email but did not click the link.You received a phishing email but did not click the link.
You clicked a link in a phishing email but did not enter information.You clicked a link in a phishing email but did not enter information.
You clicked a link in a phishing email and entered information.You clicked a link in a phishing email and entered information.
You received a voicemail that you suspect was created using Deepfake technology.You received a voicemail that you suspect was created using Deepfake technology.

You clicked a link in a phishing email and entered information.

You received a voicemail that you suspect was created using Deepfake technology.

What contact method(s) can scammers use to trick you to get around MFA and gain access to your account?(Select all that apply)
EmailEmail
Phone callPhone call
Text messageText message
Ping on TeamsPing on Teams

Email
Phone call
Text message

Where is it appropriate to save and store passwords?(Select all that apply)
Internet browsers.Internet browsers.
An unencrypted personal solution (e.g., personal Google doc).An unencrypted personal solution (e.g., personal Google doc).
A reputable password manager.A reputable password manager.
A physical notebook.A physical notebook.

A reputable password manager.
A physical notebook.

When should you text “STOP” to stop receiving messages from an automated sender?(Select all that apply)
The text comes from a known reputable source that you no longer wish to receive texts from.The text comes from a known reputable source that you no longer wish to receive texts from.
The text comes from an unknown source, and you do not want to receive additional texts from them.The text comes from an unknown source, and you do not want to receive additional texts from them.
Anytime you receive a text that prompts you to respond with “STOP” to stop receiving messages.Anytime you receive a text that prompts you to respond with “STOP” to stop receiving messages.
It is never appropriate to text “STOP” to stop receiving messages.It is never appropriate to text “STOP” to stop receiving messages.

The text comes from a known reputable source that you no longer wish to receive texts from.
The text comes from an unknown source, and you do not want to receive additional texts from them.
Anytime you receive a text that prompts you to respond with “STOP” to stop receiving messages.

You receive a text message from an unknown sender claiming to be an IT support technician from Accenture requesting access to your account to perform a critical workstation update. What should you do next?(Select all that apply)
Respond to the text message asking for additional details about the update.Respond to the text message asking for additional details about the update.
Click the link in the text message to sign into your account immediately.Click the link in the text message to sign into your account immediately.
Respond to the text message with your availability for the update to occur.Respond to the text message with your availability for the update to occur.
Delete the text message and block the number.Delete the text message and block the number.
Delete the text message and block the number.

An unknown caller requests that you navigate to a website and provide personal information to resolve an issue with your credit card. What should you do?(Select all that apply)
Navigate to the website provided by the caller and enter the requested information.Navigate to the website provided by the caller and enter the requested information.
End the call and verify the request through your credit card provider’s website or customer service phone number.End the call and verify the request through your credit card provider’s website or customer service phone number.
Navigate to the website provided by the caller and evaluate the legitimacy of the web page before deciding what to do next.Navigate to the website provided by the caller and evaluate the legitimacy of the web page before deciding what to do next.
Provide your personal information over the phone to resolve the issue.Provide your personal information over the phone to resolve the issue.
End the call and verify the request through your credit card provider’s website or customer service phone number.
